focus: This 8 PM MDT run uses 2026-06-29 as the latest Garmin day with usable sleep/HRV/readiness; however Garmin’s latest usable stats stop at 09:17 local, and Jun 30 UTC endpoints are mostly null because Denver is still on Jun 29.

Primary recovery call: readiness improved to 50/Moderate with sleep score 79, Body Battery 68 at last sync, low stress avg 18, and RHR 60 vs 66 avg; keep the evening easy because HRV remains Unbalanced and data is stale after the morning sync.
Latest movement output through sync: 632 steps, 0.53 km, 8 active kcal, 1048 total kcal, 0 intensity minutes.
Training context: Productive status, VO₂ max stable around 43.0–43.3, acute load 235, chronic load 299, ACWR 0.7 Low; prior week intensity was already 397/150 min.
Caution: next sleep need remains 9h, HRV status is still Unbalanced, SpO₂ daily low was 85, and Garmin has no evening Body Battery/stress after 09:17 local.
Nutrition log latest manual entry remains 2026-06-03: ~820 kcal, ~82g protein, water not logged; Garmin calories consumed/hydration are not populated.
Rollback to a sanitized public view is scheduled for July 3, 2026.
displayNotes: Evening runs should prefer same-local-day Garmin data, but when the endpoint is stale, label the last local sync time explicitly.
Use cards for scanability on iPhone: one primary number, status pill, and 2–3 details.
Use baseline/range visualization for HRV and RHR because personal baseline matters more than population comparison.
Use progress bars for readiness, sleep score, Body Battery, stress, steps, intensity minutes, training load, and nutrition.

Source fields include training readiness, HRV baseline, sleep score/duration/stages, Body Battery wake/current/charge/drain, stress average/max/rest split, steps/distance/active calories, weekly intensity minutes, acute load/ACWR, VO₂ max, nutrition calories/protein/water, resting HR vs 7-day average, SpO₂, and respiration.
